subject: Public Health
title: Attack Rates in Outbreaks
description: Learn Attack Rates in Outbreaks in one minute.

hook.text: At a wedding, 120 guests ate the chicken—40 got sick. 80 ate the fish—2 got sick. The chicken did it.

buildup.text: Attack rates measure the proportion of exposed people who become ill—essential for identifying outbreak sources.

discovery.text: Comparing attack rates between those who ate specific foods pinpoints the contaminated item.

twist.text: Attack rates also reveal disease transmissibility—measles has an attack rate of 90% among unvaccinated contacts.

climax.text: In foodborne outbreak investigations, attack rate analysis is often the fastest route to the source.

punchline.text: The numbers don't lie. Compare the rates, find the culprit.

quiz.question: What does an attack rate measure?
options: The proportion of exposed people who become ill
The total number of deaths from an outbreak
How fast a disease spreads geographically
